verdict_reasoning: TGN46/TGN38 is a type I integral membrane protein predominantly localized to the trans-Golgi network, but its defining biology is continuous cycling between TGN, endosomes, and the plasma membrane. A documented minority surface pool reaches the cell surface during this cycling itinerary before rapid internalization via its cytoplasmic tail signals — surface biotinylation and anti-ectodomain antibody uptake on intact cells routinely detect it. The TM topology places the ectodomain on the outer leaflet during the PM stage. Dominant compartment is TGN/endosomal, so not `yes`, but the cycling PM pool with its own TM anchor qualifies for dual_localization.
